testPage.details: Test page contains 6 block instances with various configurations (automatic/manual post selection, different post limits, program filtering)

build.details: Build completed successfully with only expected asset size warnings (editor.js 325 KiB)

lint.details: No new lint errors introduced. Existing warnings are project-wide experimental API usage unrelated to this change.

registration.details: block.json is valid with apiVersion 3, all required files exist, attributes are properly defined

implementation.details: The pt-(--admin-bar-height) class has been correctly added to the video modal container on line 113 of TestimonialsCarousel.php. This uses Tailwind v4 arbitrary property syntax which is consistent with other custom property usage in the codebase (e.g., before:-inset-x-(--side-gutter), top-(--fixed-offset)).

cssVariable.details: The --admin-bar-height CSS variable is properly defined in resources/css/screen/spacing.css (lines 35-36). It defaults to 0rem for logged-out users and uses var(--wp-admin--admin-bar--height) when WordPress admin bar is present.

tsxPhpSync.details: This is a PHP-only change. The video modal is not rendered in the TSX editor component (uses ServerSideRender), only on the frontend via PHP template. No TSX sync required.

accessibility.details: Video modal has proper ARIA attributes: role="dialog", aria-modal="true", aria-hidden="true", aria-label for the modal and close button. The modal structure is semantically correct.

functionalVerification.details: All 6 block instances on the test page successfully render with video modals present (verified via taktician_page_audit). The modal will now account for WordPress admin bar height when centering the video, preventing the video from being obscured by the admin bar for logged-in users.

passedChecks: Test page contains 6 block instances with valid block markers
Build compiles without TypeScript or compilation errors
Block is properly registered with valid block.json (apiVersion 3)
All required files exist (block.json, index.tsx, TestimonialsCarousel.tsx, TestimonialsCarousel.php)
Attributes are properly defined in block.json
The pt-(--admin-bar-height) class is correctly added to the video modal container
Tailwind v4 arbitrary property syntax is correct and consistent with codebase patterns
CSS variable --admin-bar-height is properly defined with safe defaults
Variable defaults to 0rem for logged-out users, preventing layout issues
Variable uses WordPress core --wp-admin--admin-bar--height when admin bar is present
TSX/PHP sync is not required (PHP-only change, modal not in editor)
Video modal has proper ARIA attributes (role, aria-modal, aria-label)
All 6 test page block instances render successfully with video modals
output.qaInstructions: The implementation is complete and correct. When a logged-in admin opens a testimonial video modal, the video will now be centered in the viewport area below the admin bar, rather than being obscured by it. The padding-top approach keeps the inset-0 backdrop overlay covering the full viewport (including behind the admin bar), while shifting the flex centering context downward by the admin bar height.
notes: This is a CSS-only fix that addresses admin bar overlap for video modals
The solution is safe for all user states (defaults to 0rem for logged-out users)
The syntax pt-(--admin-bar-height) is Tailwind v4 arbitrary property syntax
This pattern is already used elsewhere in the codebase for custom properties
No design QA needed - this is a functional spacing fix, not a visual design change
